script_text: Every story has three big pieces.
Characters. The people or animals in the story.
Setting. Where and when the story takes place.
Characters are the who of the story.
Listen. The kind old woman fed the birds every morning.
Who is the character? The kind old woman!
What is she like? Kind and caring. We can tell by what she does.
The setting is where and when it happens.
The old woman lived by the sea, in a tiny cottage. That is the setting!
Events are what happens in the story.
Feeding the birds every morning. That is an event!
When you read, picture the character, the setting, and the events in your mind.
Now you are ready to practice finding story parts!
